content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                    322-5901              322-6441              322-6444

November 28, 1977
                                                                                77-11-078

Mr. Norman E. Hill
Assistant Secretary
Resources Agency
1416 Ninth Street, Room 1311
Sacramento, California  95814

                    Re:  Opinion Request No. 77-037

Dear Mr. Hill:

     Thank you for your letter of October 26, 1977, request-
ing an opinion with respect to the duties of the Secretary
for Resources.  Because your request does not raise a major
question of interpretation under the Political Reform Act,
no formal opinion will be issued.  I trust, however, that
the following informal comments will provide a solution.

     Specifically, you ask whether Huey Johnson's actions
with respect to land acquisition proposals by the Depart-
ment of Parks and Recreation involve a conflict of interest
in light of Mr. Johnson's participation in the acquisition
of the same property in his capacity as an employee of the
Trust for Public Lands.

                                                                1/
     Government Code Section 87100 states the general prohi-
bition as follows:

     No public official at any level of state
or local government shall make, participate
in making or in any way attempt to use his
official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to
know he has a financial interest.

_____________________________

1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise indicated.

Norman E. Hill
November 28, 1977                                                Page Two


     Section 87103 provides in pertinent part:

          An official has a financial interest in a
          decision within the meaning of Section 87100
          if it is reasonably foreseeable that the de-
          cision will have a material financial effect,
          distinguishable from its effect on the public
          generally, on:

                         .....

               (c) Any source of income, other than loans
          by a commercial lending institution in the
          regular course of business, aggregating two
          hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
          received by or promised to the public official
          within twelve months prior to the time when
          the decision is made;.....


     The Commission has provided by Regulation 18704 that:

          Source of income, as used in Government Code
          Section 87103(c), shall not include a former
          employer if: All income from the employer was
          received by or accrued to the public official
          prior to the time he or she became a public
          official; the income was received in the nor-
          mal course of the previous employment; and
          there was no expectation by the public offi-
          cial at the time he or she assumed office of
          renewed employment with the former employer.

     Accordingly, if all of the provisions set forth in the
Regulation are satisfied, the Trust for Public Lands will not
be a source of income to Mr. Johnson, even though he received
salary from that organization in the past twelve months while
serving as its President.  Moreover, Mr. Johnson's interest
in vested pension is deemed to be previously earned salary
pursuant to the Commission's ruling in the opinion requested
by Richard J. Moore, 3 FPPC Ops. 33 (No. 76-074, March 1,
1977).  Accordingly, if the Trust for Public Lands is not a
source of income to Mr. Johnson, he is not disqualified from
making or participating in decisions which would foreseeably
and materially affect the Trust for Public Lands.

Norman E. Hill
November 28, 1977                                                Page Three


     If you have any further questions, please do not
hesitate to contact Alfredo Flores of the Conflicts of
Interest Division.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [Signature]

                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director


MB:TP:gb

sections.conclusion: If all of the provisions set forth in Regulation 18704 are satisfied (all income from the former employer was received prior to becoming a public official, in the normal course of employment, with no expectation of renewed employment), the Trust for Public Lands will not be a source of income to Mr. Johnson. sections.question_synthetic: Does Huey Johnson, Secretary for Resources, have a conflict of interest in making decisions regarding land acquisition by the Department of Parks and Recreation based on his prior employment with the Trust for Public Lands, which is involved in acquiring the same property?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that Mr. Johnson is not disqualified from making decisions affecting the Trust for Public Lands if his prior income from that organization qualifies as previously earned salary under Regulation 18704, which excludes former employers under certain conditions.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an informal opinion letter (not a formal opinion) addressing Opinion Request No. 77-037. The document contains clear question and conclusion sections. Minor OCR artifacts noted but do not significantly impact extraction quality.

no formal opinion will be issued. 
I trust, however, that 
the following informal comments will provide a solution. 
Specifically, you ask whether Huey Johnson's actions 
with respect to land acquisition proposals by the Depart-
ment of Parks and Recreation involve a conflict of interest 
in light of Mr. Johnson's participation in the acquisition 
of the same property in his capacity as an employee of the 
Trust for Public Lands. 
1/ 
Government Code Section 87l00-states the general prohi-
bition as follows: 
No public official at any level of state

1/ 322-6444 


Norman E. Hill 
November 28, 1977 
Section 87103 provides in pertinent part: 
Page Two 
An official has a financial interest in a 
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the de-
cision will have a material financial effect,

generally, on:

The Commission has provided by Regulation 18704 that: 
Source of income, as used in Government Code 
Section 87l03(c}, shall not include a former 
employer if: 
All income from the employer was 
received by or accrued to the public official 
prior to the time he or she became a public 
official; the income was received in the nor-
mal course of the previous employment; and 
there was no expectation by the public offi-
cial at the time he or she assumed office of 
renewed employment with the former employer. 
Accordingly, if all of the provisions set forth in the

serving as its President. 
Moreover, Mr. Johnson's interest 
in a vested pension is deemed to be previously earned salary

Accordingly, if the Trust for Public Lands is not a 
source of income to Mr. Johnson, he is not disqualified from

and materially affect the Trust for Public Lands. 


Norman E. Hill 
November 28, 1977 
Page Three

CONCLUSION: If all of the provisions set forth in Regulation 18704 are satisfied (all income from the former employer was received prior to becoming a public official, in the normal course of employment, with no expectation of renewed employment), the Trust for Public Lands will not be a source of income to Mr. Johnson. Accordingly, if the Trust for Public Lands is not a source of income to Mr. Johnson, he is not disqualified from making or participating in decisions which would foreseeably and materially affect the Trust for Public Lands.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provides informal guidance on whether Huey Johnson has a conflict of interest in making land acquisition decisions affecting the Trust for Public Lands, his former employer, and concludes he is not disqualified if certain conditions regarding his prior employment income are satisfied.
